title: Operational-scenario generator
phase: research

tool: clara
task: draft an operational scenario from operator research and capability brief
expectedOutput: >-
  A full operational scenario covering operator, mission context, environment,
  sequence, decisions, success/failure modes, and capability impact.
inputsFrom:
  - research-synthesiser
planeContext:
  inputs:
    - what: >-
        Operator research — interviews, field/exercise observations, workshop
        outputs
      description: >-
        Field-note work items under the `Field-notes` node (or work items with
        'interview', 'observation', 'exercise', 'field-notes', 'workshop' in
        titles). Restrict to this programme's Plane project.
    - what: Capability brief or statement of operational need
      description: >-
        Work items under *Briefs*, *Capability*, *Mission* (or with
        'capability-brief', 'operational-need', 'mission-statement' in titles).
    - what: Doctrinal or procedural references the operators work from (optional)
      description: >-
        Work items under *Doctrine*, *Procedures*, *Standards* (or with
        'doctrine', 'procedure', 'TTP' in titles).
    - what: Themes (optional)
      description: >-
        Themes section of the `Knowledge Base/{{track}}/Research-synthesis` work
        item.
    - what: Friction points (optional)
      description: >-
        Friction-points section of the `Knowledge
        Base/{{track}}/Research-synthesis` work item. The failure-modes section
        draws on this.
  outputPathTemplate: 'Knowledge Base/{{track}}/Operational scenarios/{{scenario-title}}'
visibility: public

copyBlock: Use CLARA's `operational-scenario-generator` for <programme name>.
---

## What CLARA will ask you for

- **Programme name** — your programme's name (e.g. SKYPROTECT).
- **Track** — the slice of the programme this artefact belongs to (workstream, capability area, feature line, etc.), or `Programme-wide` if it spans tracks.
- **Scenario title** — short (e.g. "Tank crew night transit through contested terrain", "Fighter aircraft low-altitude intercept", "Frigate surface-contact classification at dusk").
- **Inputs** — CLARA will search the programme's Plane project for operator research — interviews, field/exercise observations, workshop outputs; capability brief or statement of operational need; doctrinal or procedural references the operators work from (optional); themes (optional); friction points (optional). You can also paste fresh material if it's not in Plane yet.

## Where the output lands

`Knowledge Base/{{track}}/Operational scenarios/{{scenario-title}}` inside your programme's Plane project.

## Tips

- Validate the draft with at least one operator before using it in capability discussions. Their reaction is the real test.
- Pay particular attention to the failure modes — that's where the design opportunity usually hides.
- Pair the output with a visual: use [Nanobanana](/tools/nanobanana), [Luma](/tools/luma-ai), or [Forma](/tools/autodesk-forma) to make the scenario tangible.
- The output feeds the [capability-spec generator](/prompts/capability-spec-generator) and [mission-thread mapper](/prompts/mission-thread-mapper).
